suggested_fixes: Ensure tensors are 16-byte aligned (e.g., by using at::Tensor::data_ptr<float>() after verifying alignment or by copying to aligned buffers).
Compute tmp = at::matmul(B.transpose(0,1), X) (16×d) and then Y += at::matmul(A, tmp) for a significantly more efficient implementation.
warnings: Float4 loads from A and B assume 16-byte alignment; if not guaranteed, undefined behavior may occur.

The fused kernel computes the dot product A[row]·B[l] redundantly for every column in the block, resulting in O(d^3) operations. Consider precomputing B.T @ X as a 16×d matrix and then multiplying by A.
